mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 10% signals a severe labor market dislocation and deep economic recession. This widespread job loss crushes consumer demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The signal fails if, when UNRATE > 10%, the average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and economic growth. This positive momentum in consumption leads to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) environment, an unexpected acceleration in inflation forces markets to price in premature monetary tightening. This increases the discount rate on future earnings and reduces equity valuations, causing negative returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if, during ZIRP periods, S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the inflation trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment indicates a severe, broad-based economic contraction. This interaction is more potent than either signal alone, confirming a recession and triggering sharp downward revisions to earnings, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show significant economic stress
failure_condition: A model using both INDPRO and UNRATE fails to show a statistically significant improvement in predictive power (e.g., via likelihood ratio test) over models using only one of the series.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key leading indicator for the broader economy due to their wide-ranging impact on construction, manufacturing, and retail. A negative trend in housing starts signals a future slowdown in economic activity, negatively impacting equity market returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST value of 891.0 is historically low
failure_condition: The 6-month moving average of HOUST changes has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 60-day forward returns that is not consistently negative (i.e., positive more than 40% of the time).

mechanism_narrative: A catastrophic monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls, such as a loss exceeding 1 million jobs, signals an abrupt halt to economic activity. This shock immediately triggers recession fears and causes a rapid repricing of risk assets, leading to sharp negative returns in the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 132,912k reflects a massive drop from pre-shock levels
failure_condition: In months following a payrolls drop of over 1 million, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is near zero, the central bank has limited tools to combat economic weakness. If real GDP growth also turns negative, this combination signals policy impotence in the face of a recession, causing a loss of confidence and driving equity markets lower.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during quarters where both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP is the technical definition of an economic contraction. This confirmation of weakening aggregate demand leads to systematic downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts, resulting in negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal is falsified if the information coefficient of quarterly GDPC1 growth on next-month S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a full business cycle.
